# LLM Wiki
> A personal knowledge compiler powered by AI. Transform raw articles into structured, interconnected wiki entries that compound over time.

## Project
- Name: LLM Wiki (obsidian-llm-wiki)
- Version: 0.4.0
- Author: gusibi
- License: MIT
- GitHub: https://github.com/gusibi/obsidian-llm-wiki

## Description
LLM Wiki is a personal knowledge base building tool that uses LLMs to compile raw articles into structured wiki entries. It has two components: an Obsidian plugin (GUI) and a Claude Code skill (CLI). Core idea: instead of RAG (retrieve-and-forget each query), the LLM reads source material once, distills it, and maintains a growing wiki with cross-references and source attribution. Knowledge compounds over time.

## Pages
- Home: https://llmwiki.eztoolab.com/ — Landing page with features, workflow, philosophy comparison
- Documentation: https://llmwiki.eztoolab.com/docs — Full installation and usage guide
- Changelog: https://llmwiki.eztoolab.com/changelog — Release history

## Core Commands
- /init: Initialize wiki structure (plugin-native, zero LLM dependency)
- /ingest: Compile articles into wiki entries (summary, concepts, entities, methods, cross-references)
- /query: Ask questions grounded in wiki content with wikilink references
- /lint: Comprehensive wiki health check (contradictions, orphans, stale content, missing attribution)
- /scan: Lightweight legacy library indexing (titles + first 10 lines only)

## Core Philosophy
- raw/ is immutable (LLM reads only, never modifies)
- wiki/ is LLM territory (humans read but don't edit directly)
- Every claim in wiki must be traceable to source (source: [[filename]])
- Contradictions are flagged with warning callouts, never silently overwritten
- Knowledge compounds: each ingest enriches the entire wiki network

## Two Components
- Obsidian Plugin: GUI inside Obsidian with unified Wiki panel + Chat panel. Communicates via ACP with Claude Code or Cursor.
- Claude Code Skill: CLI-based. Works in any directory without Obsidian. Same wiki engine, different interface.
